results.alt: " I was a student at Alexandria when the fire came. I saved seven scrolls — I chose them in the dark, by feel alone. I have never stopped wondering what I left behind. " The Library That Burned

" The reef had been dying for years. We all knew it. That morning, the wave came anyway — ten feet, glassy, perfect. We paddled out together, all twelve of us, and rode it in silence. " Surfing the Last Perfect Wave

" My caravan had crossed the Taklamakan seventeen times. On the eighteenth, a sandstorm buried three of my companions. I carried their names carved in jade all the way to Samarkand. " The Silk Road Merchant's Last Journey

" We had been separated for twenty-eight years. When the crowds broke through, I ran. I did not stop running until I found my sister standing exactly where we had last said goodbye. " The Night the Wall Came Down

" I was seven years old when my father woke me before dawn to witness the morning prayers at the river. The mist rose like spirits departing, and a thousand oil lamps floated downstream. " First Light on the Ganges

" My grandmother's hands, cracked like the earth itself, gathering the final sheaves of wheat as the sky turned the color of rust. She sang a song her mother taught her, and her mother before that. " The Last Harvest Before the Drought

" The schoolmaster had only three books for forty children. We took turns, one page each, and memorized what we could not hold. I still know those pages by heart. " Learning to Read by Candlelight

" She was born in the Artemis habitat. When she took her first steps in the low gravity, she laughed — a sound I had never heard before, pure and weightless, like she was made for this. " My Daughter's First Steps on the Moon

I was seven years old when my father woke me before dawn to witness the morning prayers at the river. The mist rose like spirits departing, and a thousand oil lamps floated downstream. First Light on the Ganges

We drove the last spike at Promontory Summit. The foreman shook my hand — the first time a white man had done so. I thought of the mountains we had blasted through, the men we had buried in them. Building the First Transcontinental Railroad
